question_text: A spring is stretched by a force of 20 N and extends by 10 cm. If the same spring is stretched by a force of 40 N, how much will it extend? (Assume the spring obeys Hooke's Law and stays elastic.)
explanation: Using Hooke's Law, force is proportional to extension (F = ke). If 20 N causes 10 cm extension, then doubling the force to 40 N will double the extension to 20 cm. The spring constant (k) stays the same, so the extension increases proportionally with the applied force.
